title: In memory, December 2011
summary: Donn Brian Baker, 59 Donn Brian Baker died Sept. 7 at the Rockingham Assisted Living Home in Anchorage. Mr. Baker was born March 8, 1952, in Barrow, Alaska. He is survived by his daughter, Debra Francine Huck; and sisters, D’Brianne P. Friemering and Donna Barnhart. title: 2012 dividend payments
summary: CIRI’s dividend policy states that the total CIRI dividend payment to shareholders in any given year is equal to 3.50 percent of total shareholders’ equity, calculated as of Dec. 31 of the prior year. summary: CIRI shareholder Tom Gerds celebrates his business’ second year of operation this December. In 2009, he opened Advanced Care Pet Hospital with his wife, Dr. Pamela Gerds, in St. Cloud, Minn. title: New book

title: Baby Valentina
summary: CIRI shareholder Linda Olguin Dean and her husband Thomas Dean excitedly announce the birth of their daughter, Valentina Janet Rose Dean. Baby Valentina was born July 1. She is the baby sister of Nathaniel Dean. The post Baby Valentina first appeared on CIRI .

title: Stig Colberg now CIRI CFO
summary: CIRI named Stig Colberg chief financial officer effective Dec. 19, 2011. Colberg joined CIRI in 2008 and was the Company’s chief business development officer. title: A word from the president:  40 years of ANCSA
summary: By: Margie Brown, CIRI president and CEO As the year 2011 comes to a close, I wish you the best of the holiday season. For me, this year passed very quickly. I find it remarkable how rapidly it seems the last four decades have passed by. This month, we commemorate 40 years of opportunity for Alaska Native people with the passage the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA). ANCSA was enacted on Dec. 18, 1971. It settled Alaska Native peoples’ aboriginal land claims and distributed 44 m...

title: Remembering the Cook Inlet Land Exchange
summary: “The Cook Inlet Land Exchange was pivotal to CIRI’s history,” reflected Margie Brown, CIRI president and CEO. “ANCSA held the promise that Alaska Native people would retain lands of like and similar character to lands traditionally used. title: Celebrating 40 years of ANCSA
summary: Dec. 18 marked the 40th anniversary of the signing of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA).
